file_name: NETGEAR,INC_04_21_2003-EX-10.16-AMENDMENT TO THE DISTRIBUTOR AGREEMENT BETWEEN INGRAM MICRO AND NETGEAR.txt

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without prior written permission from the other party, which permission shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Any attempt by either party to assign any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement without such permission will be voidable.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without prior written permission from the other party, which permission shall be granted at the sole discretion of the other party. Any attempt by either party to assign any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement without such permission will be immediately null and void.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, assignment required permission that 'shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed'. The modification states permission shall be granted at the 'sole discretion' of the other party, fundamentally changing the condition under which assignment can occur. This creates a contradiction because one clause implies a reasonable standard, while the other grants absolute power of denial. It also changes the effect of unpermitted assignment; originally 'voidable' (at the discretion of the non-assigning party) now 'immediately null and void.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by either party without prior written permission from the other party, which permission shall be granted at the sole discretion of the other party. Any attempt by either party to assign any right, or delegate any duty or obligation which arises under the Agreement without such permission will be immediately null and void.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces a clear contradiction. The original text stated permission for assignment 'shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed,' implying a reasonable standard for granting permission. The changed text grants permission at the 'sole discretion' of the other party, giving them absolute power to deny assignment, negating any obligation to be reasonable. This directly contradicts the original provision by replacing a reasonableness standard with unrestricted discretion. Furthermore, the original text made unpermitted assignment 'voidable', while the modified text makes it 'immediately null and void,' changing the legal effect of such an action. The contradicted text is the Changed Text and they are exactly the same, reinforcing that a contradiction was introduced.

perturbation.original_text: The terms and conditions of this Amendment, shall amend and supersede any conflicting terms of the original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement shall remain unchanged.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms and conditions of this Amendment, shall amend and supersede only the directly conflicting terms of the original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement, including those indirectly affected, shall also be amended to reflect the spirit and intent of this amendment.
perturbation.explanation: The change creates an in-text contradiction. The original states that 'All other terms of the original Agreement shall remain unchanged.' The modified text now says that 'All other terms of the original Agreement, including those indirectly affected, shall also be amended to reflect the spirit and intent of this amendment.' This contradicts the earlier statement by implying that other terms *do* change, even if indirectly. This creates uncertainty about which terms are valid and how broadly the amendment should be applied, leading to potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms and conditions of this Amendment, shall amend and supersede only the directly conflicting terms of the original Agreement. All other terms of the original Agreement, including those indirectly affected, shall also be amended to reflect the spirit and intent of this amendment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction as it expands the scope of amendment beyond 'directly conflicting terms' to include 'those indirectly affected,' contradicting the initial assertion that 'All other terms of the original Agreement shall remain unchanged.' This inconsistency creates uncertainty and potential disputes regarding which terms are valid and the extent of the amendment's application, thereby satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties,' leading to disputes over contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Following the [*], for any extension or renewal term, Distributor's appointment as Distributor shall be non-exclusive and NETGEAR may appoint other distributors in [*] at its sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: Following the [*], for any extension or renewal term, Distributor's appointment as Distributor shall be non-exclusive and NETGEAR may appoint other distributors in [*]; however, NETGEAR must first offer the distributorship to Ingram Micro at terms no less favorable than offered to other distributors.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text adds a condition on NETGEAR's ability to appoint other distributors, by first offering it to Ingram Micro. This directly contradicts the phrase 'at its sole discretion', which implies NETGEAR can appoint anyone without needing to get approval or offer terms to Ingram Micro, causing an in-text contradiction and uncertainty about distributor appointment procedures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Following the [*], for any extension or renewal term, Distributor's appointment as Distributor shall be non-exclusive and NETGEAR may appoint other distributors in [*]; however, NETGEAR must first offer the distributorship to Ingram Micro at terms no less favorable than offered to other distributors.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants NETGEAR 'sole discretion' in appointing distributors. The changed text introduces a condition that NETGEAR must first offer the distributorship to Ingram Micro, directly conflicting with the 'sole discretion' clause as it restricts NETGEAR's freedom in distributor appointments. The Contradicted Text is in the same location, and because the Changed Text modifies a key clause, it causes the in-text contradiction. This creates a strong contradiction because it is unclear whether NETGEAR can truly appoint distributors at its sole discretion.
